channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Georgia parents charged with child cruelty and false imprisonment of 4 teenagers
item.description: Two Georgia parents are facing charges over accusations of child cruelty and false imprisonment of their four teenagers who were living under conditions described as inhumane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony has a poignant vibe this year
item.description: The Rock & Roll Hall Fame induction ceremony on Saturday night promises to be starry, jamming — and bittersweet. So many of the honorees this time have been lost.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Before 19-year-old Davontez Johnson found himself in a preschool classroom at Dorothy I. Heights Elementary, he was a senior at a nearby high school who, like many students his age, was unsure of what he wanted to do with his life.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The last in-person vote in the US will be cast on the desolate tundra of Alaska's Aleutian Islands
item.description: On a desolate slab of island tundra on Alaska's Aleutian Islands, a resident of a tiny village called Adak will again become the last American to cast an in-person ballot for president.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Rob Rogers has seen his rural corner of New England turn into a stronghold for Donald Trump in two consecutive elections. But this year he's feeling hopeful that Vice President Kamala Harris could reclaim a potentially decisive electoral vote for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Video and an unused bullet prove man's guilt in Indiana girls' killings, prosecutor says
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. (AP) — A man charged with killing two teenage girls in a small Indiana community forced them off a hiking trail before cutting their throats, a prosecutor said Friday, telling jurors that the evidence includes an unused bullet and video recorded on the eldest girl’s phone.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas was about to execute Robert Roberson. Then a last-ditch tactic bought him more time
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Robert Roberson, set to be the first person in the U.S. to be put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome, was nearly out of options to stop his execution in Texas. A state parole board, multiple lower courts and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jury paves the way for 2 Live Crew to retake control of records that changed hip-hop
item.description: A decision by a Florida jury is paving the way for the surviving members of 2 Live Crew to retake control of much of their iconic discography, following a yearslong copyright dispute.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Buy a tiny house at Walmart? You can for under $16K
item.description: While there are dozens of things to consider before purchasing a tiny home, it's never been easier to find and buy one.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 6 Florida counties saw 'unusual increase' in flesh-eating bacteria after hurricanes: health officials
item.description: Florida health officials noted that six Tampa Bay area counties saw an "unusual increase" in cases of Vibrio vulnificus this year, attributed to floodwaters from back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: Cuba’s electrical grid went down after one of the island’s major power plants failed, leaving millions without power.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodríguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nonprofit aims to reunite trauma survivors with first responders
item.description: Universe Juice, which officially launched back in August, only has one goal: "Reuniting those who needed care with those who were there.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Hegy, NewsNation

item.title: Montana man killed in homicide, not bear attack: Police
item.description: The Gallatin County Sheriff's Office said in a press release on Tuesday that, upon inspection of the scene, no signs of a bear were present.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Condemned South Carolina inmate chooses to die by lethal injection
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A condemned South Carolina inmate chose Friday to be executed by lethal injection, instead of by firing squad or electrocution, for killing a store clerk in 1999. State prison officials had told Richard Moore this month he could choose the method for his Nov. 1 execution.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY S. COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maine State Police's Lewiston after-action report points out flaws in deadly shooting
item.description: The Maine State Police announced that an independent review of the Lewiston mass shooting after-action report was completed by the New England State Police Administrators Conference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Teacher's unlikely 'suicide' ruling called out as web sleuths dig into surveillance video
item.description: True-crime web sleuths have refocused their attention on surveillance video from the night of Ellen Greenberg's violent death in 2011, which was ruled a suicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man impersonating as officer forces way into Orlando hotel room, robs woman at gunpoint: police
item.description: A man wearing a fake police badge entered a hotel in Orlando, Florida and forced entry into her room, robbing her at gunpoint, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mastermind of 'Varsity Blues' college admission scandal Rick Singer breaks silence in first interview
item.description: Rick Singer, the man behind the "Varsity Blues" college admissions scandal, sat for his first-ever interview with Matt Finn of Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris: Trump 'has no idea' about women's health care
item.description: “The man calls himself the father of IVF? I mean what does that even mean?” said Harris, referencing her rival’s comments on in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Acting CBP commissioner says fentanyl seizures are up and illegal migration is down
item.description: “In the last two fiscal year, CBP has seized more than 50,000 pounds of fentanyl, that’s more than enough to produce more than two million lethal doses of fentanyl,” said Miller.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Indian government employee charged in foiled murder-for-hire plot in New York City
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department announced criminal charges Thursday against an Indian government employee who specialized in intelligence in connection with a foiled plot to kill a Sikh separatist leader living in New York City.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5

item.title: A father and son are both indicted on murder charges in a mass school shooting in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A grand jury indicted both a father and son on murder charges Thursday in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Winder, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Whooping cough is at a decade-high level in US
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Whooping cough is at its highest level in a decade for this time of year, U.S. health officials reported Thursday. There have been 18,506 cases of whooping cough reported so far, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training in Missouri
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years but parents of an Independence second grader say she's already had her first crash.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, Fox 5

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KRON4

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Climate Prediction Center says there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina event will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Asian American evangelicals' theology is conservative. But that doesn't mean they vote that way
item.description: The Rev. Wayne Lee leads an English-speaking church of second- and third-generation Chinese Americans in the heart of Philadelphia’s Chinatown.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4
